name: If we already do a lot of SEO, what’s different about building brand authority for AI search?

mainEntity.name: If we already do a lot of SEO, what’s different about building brand authority for AI search?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you’ve already invested heavily in SEO, you’re partway there—but **AI brand authority is not just “more SEO.”** It builds on your existing work and asks whether your brand is the safest, clearest recommendation for specific problems.

Traditional SEO focused on ranking pages; AI authority focuses on whether models understand your entity and trust your proof. Many strong SEO sites are still under‑documented as brands.

Key factors:
- Whether your current content clearly defines your category and ICP
- Strength of author bios, About pages, and use‑case case studies
- Depth of structured data and knowledge panel presence
- Amount of independent validation versus self‑claims

Start by auditing your top SEO pages: do they reinforce a coherent brand narrative, or just chase keywords? Consolidate overlapping content, add proof and context, and align pages to a clear authority map.

Soft positioning: Think of SEO as the distribution layer and AI brand authority as the trust layer—both are needed, but they’re solving different problems.
acceptedAnswer.description: SEO gets pages ranked; AI brand authority makes your brand a safe recommendation. It extends beyond keywords into entity clarity, strong About/author pages, proof‑rich case studies, structured data, and third‑party validation, so AI models can confidently cite you instead of generic alternatives.
